title: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
summary: NASA is finalizing science plans for observations to be conducted by astronauts during the Artemis 2 lunar flyby mission. The team is preparing detailed objectives for the upcoming mission.

title: Atlas 5 launches Amazon Leo satellites
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket successfully launched a new batch of Amazon's Project Kuiper broadband satellites on April 4. The mission aims to speed up the deployment of Amazon's low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellation.

title: Space Force budget would more than double in Trump’s $1.5 trillion defense plan
summary: The proposed U.S. defense budget for fiscal year 2027 includes a $71 billion request for the Space Force, more than doubling its funding. Over $60 billion of this would go toward procurement, research, and development.

title: Artemis 2 in good shape cruising towards the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft is performing well with only minor issues after its engine burn to head to the moon. The mission is currently on track as it cruises toward the lunar destination.

title: Italy’s Argotec plans to scale Florida satellite facility to meet rising US demand
summary: Argotec has opened its first U.S. satellite production facility near Kennedy Space Center in Florida to meet increasing demand from American space programs. The expansion aims to strengthen Italy's presence in the growing U.S. space industry.

title: White House again proposes steep NASA budget cuts
summary: The White House has proposed significant budget cuts for NASA for the second year in a row, which would heavily impact the agency's science programs and the International Space Station.

title: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments
summary: Amazon announced it will revise its broadband satellite constellation deployment plans after SpaceX raised concerns about potential space safety risks. Amazon denied SpaceX's claims, leading to a public dispute between the two companies.

title: Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’
summary: Europe's role in the 'second space age' hinges on achieving strategic autonomy in space technology, reducing reliance on external sources. The European Union emphasizes the need for independent access, control, and capabilities in space.

title: China’s commercial Tianlong-3 rocket fails on debut launch
summary: China's commercial Tianlong-3 rocket, developed by Space Pioneer, failed during its maiden launch due to an anomaly during ascent. The debut flight ended in failure, marking a setback for the private space sector.

title: Artemis 2 heads to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has launched toward the moon following a successful maneuver on April 2. This marks a significant step in the agency's lunar exploration program.

title: The powerful new Rubin Observatory just found 11,000 new asteroids and measured 'tens of thousands more'
summary: The Rubin Observatory has discovered 11,000 new asteroids and measured tens of thousands more, providing early insights into the expected surge in discoveries once full operations commence.

title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts will try to recreate Apollo 8's historic 'Earthrise' photo during April 6 flyby
summary: The Artemis 2 crew plans to recreate Apollo 8's iconic 'Earthrise' photo during an April 6 flyby. This mission aims to honor the historic 1968 image taken during the Apollo program.

title: Artemis 2 astronaut Victor Glover delivers inspiring Easter message on the way to the moon (video)
summary: NASA astronaut Victor Glover shared an impromptu Easter message during the Artemis 2 mission as the Orion capsule traveled toward the moon. The event took place on April 4.

title: Dozens of hidden star streams found in the outskirts of our Milky Way galaxy
summary: Astronomers discovered dozens of hidden stellar streams in the outskirts of the Milky Way using Gaia data, providing new insights into galaxy formation and dark matter.

title: Lego's educational space sets are a stellar way to teach kids about the basic physics of collisions and impacts
summary: Lego's educational space-themed sets are highlighted as an engaging tool for teaching children about physics concepts like collisions and impacts. The article emphasizes the importance of making science accessible and fun for kids.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts — now halfway to the moon — report 'burning smell' from toilet, but everything's fine
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts reported a burning smell from the Orion spacecraft's toilet, but NASA Mission Control downplayed concerns. The crew is now over halfway to the moon.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 204 — A New NASA
summary: Episode 204 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses the ongoing Artemis 2 mission from Houston. The episode features hosts Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: Disclosure day: If ET made contact, how would we handle the news?
summary: Experts discuss potential reactions and protocols if extraterrestrial life made contact with Earth. Space.com gathered diverse opinions on how humanity would handle a real-life 'disclosure day' regarding alien contact.

title: Defender at 45: How an icon of space gaming is in danger of being lost to history
summary: The iconic arcade shooter 'Defender' turns 45 in 2026, raising concerns about its preservation for its 50th anniversary. The game's legacy is at risk of being lost to history without proper conservation efforts.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in a critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to carry astronauts around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: NASASpaceFlight.com reports that Artemis II will return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The article references the Apollo 17 mission as a historical milestone in lunar exploration.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets, with liftoffs planned from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights advancements in spaceflight technology and deep-space mission capabilities.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit aboard the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has postponed the second launch of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed the plan.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to Starbase launch pads to support the upcoming Block 3 Starship configuration, aiming to increase launch frequency. The activity highlights SpaceX's focus on enhancing operational efficiency at its Texas facility.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a booklet celebrating 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. This publication highlights key milestones and contributions by the agency in the space sector.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed with multifunctional capabilities, potentially revolutionizing space applications. This innovation could streamline satellite and spacecraft systems by integrating multiple functions into a single chip.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational and communicating normally.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting industry events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber, with launch planned for January 2027 aboard an Ariane 6 rocket. The spacecraft's 26 ultrasensitive cameras are being exposed to extreme temperatures and vacuum conditions to validate functionality before deployment.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use the new costing software in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference while being cost-effective and simple to manufacture. The innovation addresses the growing risks of disruptions to critical satellite-based technologies.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same top-line funding as the 2026 request. The proposal includes cuts to NASA's science and STEM missions, similar to the previous year's budget.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper'. The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and data points defining the space industry in Q1 2026, with a focus on the resurgence of Earth Observation (EO) capabilities. It highlights significant developments and metrics shaping the sector during this period.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a vision for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed landings starting next year, sparking excitement among CLPS companies. The initiative, dubbed an 'Opportunity Bomb,' presents a significant opportunity for lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with four astronauts, including the first woman and person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The mission marks a historic step in space exploration.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding. The company aims to build an uncrewed space station to host manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has secured $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with additional investment from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other new investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base and sees it as a strategic step for future Mars missions. The comments highlight growing congressional backing for NASA's Artemis program.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager Space and Icarus Robotics are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS). This partnership highlights advancements in space robotics technology.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching its Iridium NTN Direct service, which CEO Matt Desch claims will be the first standardized global narrow-band satellite IoT service. The company is also advancing alternative PNT (Positioning, Navigation, and Timing) solutions.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has secured a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for Europe under the ESA's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass has historically posed challenges for satellite services, but demand is now growing as of September 2025.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The potential deal could expand Amazon's satellite and connectivity services.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. This funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force’s innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate to develop a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing. The partnership aims to create a capability for safely removing satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies are urging the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency (SDA) acknowledges the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada's Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed constellation for broadband services. Northwestel operates in Canada's northern regions, including Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation and vertical integration strategy.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation signed a memorandum of understanding to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to advance satellite-based IoT services.
